title: Malty Tenders Argentinian Empanada Bake
description: Tender, flaky empanadas filled with a sweet and spicy beef mixture, served with a side of malty malbec-glazed onions. Perfect for a weekend brunch.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Combine beef, sriracha, diced pickle, malty malbec wine, chopped onion, salt, black pepper, and baking powder in a bowl and mix well.
Roll out dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Brush edges with beaten eggs and fold dough over filling to form a half-circle shape.
Press edges with a fork to seal.
Place empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Glaze onions with melted butter and malty malbec wine, then sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve empanadas with onions on the side.
Adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve warm, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
Reheat in a 350°F (180°C) oven for 10-15 minutes or until warmed through.
Serve with a side of malty malbec-glazed onions.
Glaze onions with melted butter and malty malbec wine, then sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve warm.
Adjust seasoning as needed.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
Reheat in a 350°F (180°C) oven for 10-15 minutes or until warmed through.
